justification: All 4 business_impact sub-fields (financial, operational, user, risk) are substantively populated with multi-sentence analysis. 7 distinct stakeholder groups identified with specific impact per group (B2C customers, B2B customers, partner integrations, operations/dispatch, customer support, sales/account management, finance/revenue). 4 escalations for missing context including contractual SLA risk explicitly. Competitive positioning mentioned in both user impact ('puts the service behind competitors who offer 1-2 hour windows') and risk ('industry trend is toward narrower windows'). Confidence level of 45 is well below the 80 threshold with clearly listed assumptions and low-confidence areas. Premium delivery tier revenue risk explicitly addressed in financial impact.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough stakeholder analysis with 7 distinct groups, far exceeding the minimum of 3, each with specific and actionable impact descriptions
Confidence level of 45 is well-calibrated — correctly reflects that contract information and business rationale are missing
4 separate escalations covering business rationale, SLA contracts, customer segmentation, and scope clarity — all with concrete recommendations
Contractual SLA risk explicitly identified with recommendation for Legal review before proceeding
Competitive positioning addressed in multiple places, noting industry trend toward narrower windows
Financial analysis goes beyond revenue to include redelivery costs ($5-15 per attempt) and premium tier downgrade risk
Operational impact balanced — acknowledges reduced pressure benefit while noting it masks root-cause prediction issues
Edge cases are strong — same-day tier incompatibility, midnight boundary, timezone ambiguity, B2B dock scheduling, and SLA reporting regime change
Rejection reasons tied to specific business rules, providing clear traceability

agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"The engineering team proposes changing delivery ETA display from exact timestamps (e.g., 'Delivery by 14:30 on Friday') to date-only windows (e.g., 'Delivery by Friday'). This is a reduction in the precision of delivery promises communicated to end users and potentially integrated partners. In logistics, ETA precision is a core competitive differentiator and directly tied to customer satisfaction, SLA enforcement, and contractual obligations.","bounded_con
